title: Romantic German Cherry Pastry
description: A delicate cherry-filled pastry with a marinated element, perfect for a romantic German-inspired dinner or dessert. This recipe avoids meat and is suitable for a vegetarian Buddhist temple-friendly meal.
instructions: Combine cherry jam, almond milk, and lemon juice in a saucepan, and heat over low heat until warm and melted.
Add in the granulated sugar and whisk until dissolved.
Add the unsalted butter and whisk until melted and smooth.
Using a stand mixer, combine the flour, yeast, and salt in a bowl, and mix on low speed.
Add the eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition.
Add the vanilla extract and mix until combined.
Gradually add in the warm jam mixture, and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough, and roll it out into a large rectangle.
Cut the dough into 8 equal pieces, and roll each piece into a ball.
Place the balls onto a greased baking sheet, and let rise for another 30 minutes.
Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Let cool for 5 minutes, then drizzle with plum jam and sprinkle with confectioners' sugar.
Mix the feta cheese with marjoram, and crumble it on top of the pastries.
Serve warm, with a scoop of vanilla ice cream if desired.
Dust with confectioners' sugar before serving.
Cut into squares or rectangles, and serve.
